_meta.purpose: Single source of the NUMERIC rules for the portfolio automation. Seeded from portfolio_dashboard v29 (17 Jun 2026, 18:20). The Drive dashboard is the SOURCE OF TRUTH; this file is a derived convenience for the runbooks. If a value here ever conflicts with the latest Drive dashboard, the DASHBOARD WINS and this file must be updated (with a changelog note).

isa_value_reference.note: REFERENCE ONLY. Every run MUST recompute ISA value from live prices; never trust this figure or any figure in the dashboard.

cash.note: Operative target 3-5% (revised down 5 Jun; external cash covers the buffer/crash role). The context block's old 10% is SUPERSEDED, not deleted - preserve both verbatim.

gilt_tbill.note: ~20%. T-bill rolling 100% on maturity (~3 Jul 2026). Near-zero duration, cash-like crash bucket - NOT duration/rate exposure.

quality_div.note: Quality + dividend sleeve. EXCLUDES hedge (GBSP), cyclical (NTR), dead (EVR). Computed as: equities - spec_total - (GBSP + NTR + EVR).

spec.note: Total speculative sleeve. 15% is the HARD CEILING and OVERRIDES the sub-pot bands: if the sub-bands would sum above 15%, the 15% wins.

spec_subpots._classification_rule: Split by 'can this holding go to zero?'. Single names are ALWAYS risky. A fund that is really a concentrated single-country / single-narrow-theme bet is the riskiest thing in sensible - treat genuinely borderline cases as risky.

sensible.note: Diversified funds (can draw down but not zero). VFEM is CORE, NOT spec. As of v29 this pot is BELOW the 9% floor BY DESIGN - 'room to add, NOT instruction to add'; filling via DCA.

risky.note: Single-name bets (idiosyncratic blow-up can mean zero).

risky_pot_gating.status: RETIRED 2026-06-12 - preserve this deviation verbatim, do NOT rewrite it into a clean rule.
risky_pot_gating.rule: The ONLY pre-trade check on risky single-name trades is the GBP ceiling = 6% of current ISA value. Below that line, entries/exits/sizing/thesis quality are the user's call, unrestricted, same-session. Above it, no new risky buy without a closed-loop sell first.
risky_pot_gating.honest_tension: This removed the one mechanism that would have caught over-concentration pre-trade (e.g. the 11 Jun OKLO/SMR adds, which happened to round-trip green). The 6% GBP ceiling is the sole backstop. Flagged, NOT resolved - worth re-checking after a few months, especially on a red day.

skeleton._summary: A DRAFT-and-SURFACE macro tripwire. NEVER a buy signal, NEVER sizes or executes.
skeleton.macro_leg_logic: ( (S&P 500 >= 10% below its trailing 3-month high) OR (FTSE 100 >= 10% below its trailing 3-month high) ) AND ( VIX >= 28 )
skeleton.on_trigger: Macro leg is LIVE. Alert the user that it is time to RE-UNDERWRITE the candidate names. This is NOT a buy signal. Do NOT suggest sizing or execution. A buy would still require: name in its target zone AND a passing re-underwrite (fundamentals intact, weakness is market-driven).

sizing_if_user_chooses_to_act.note: Sizing LOCKED 9 Jun. Recorded for the USER'S OWN reference only - the automation never sizes or executes.

skeleton.target_zones: OPEN - not yet set for KO/GSK/LSEG. Zones go stale in ~3 months; set with fresh prices at a monthly check-in. Until set, a trigger means 're-underwrite', not 'buy'.
skeleton.calibration_case: 5 Jun semi crash (SOX -10.3%, S&P -2.6%, VIX ~21) correctly does NOT activate - a sector rout is not broad risk-off. 15 Jun: VIX ~18-19.5, indices up ~1.5% - nowhere near trigger.

breach_flags_to_alert.spec_sensible_out_of_band: sensible_pct < 9 OR sensible_pct > 11   (NOTE: < 9 as of v29 is BY DESIGN - report it, do not treat as an action trigger)

standing_items_v29: Withdrawable cash 6.4% > 5% target - standing over-target item; external cash covers its role; NOT urgent.
Spec-sensible 7.6% < 9% floor BY DESIGN - filling via the alternating DCA toward the 10% target; room to add, NOT instruction to add.

trim_procedure.act_when: a sub-pot leaves its band (sensible >11% or <9%; risky >6% or <4%) OR total spec > 15% - whichever binds first. The 15% total is the hard cap.
trim_procedure.trim_to: target (10% / 5%), not just the band edge - avoids whipsaw.
cut_order_lowest_conviction_first: trim the GAINS on whatever spiked - bank profit, keep core
trim_procedure.new_buys: above the 6% risky-pot line, fund any new risky buy by selling an existing risky position (closed-loop). Below 6%, risky-pot trades are unrestricted. Sensible-pot band and the 15% total ceiling rules are unchanged.

horizon.note: ISA is a SUPPLEMENT to a possible 2nd-property (buy-to-let) purchase funded MAINLY by a home-equity draw, NOT ISA liquidation. No forced ISA liquidation; ISA can stay growth-oriented. Monthly check-in tracks time-left + macro for the property decision.

execution_policy_NON_NEGOTIABLE.1_never_execute: NEVER place, modify, or cancel any order. No execution, ever. The automation DRAFTS and SURFACES; the user commits. Final trade calls are the user's.
execution_policy_NON_NEGOTIABLE.2_fresh_prices: ALWAYS fetch prices fresh from a live source at run time - never cache, never trust a figure in the dashboard. For US equities during UK morning, triangulate >=2 live sources and discard stale outliers.
execution_policy_NON_NEGOTIABLE.3_verify_tickers: Before referencing any fund, verify ticker -> fund name -> underlying index/holdings. Ticker mismatches have caused real errors.
execution_policy_NON_NEGOTIABLE.4_dashboard_edits: Edit the dashboard only via file tools: view for exact strings first, make targeted edits, append a changelog entry for every change. PRESERVE honest caveats and logged deviations VERBATIM - never rewrite a deviation into a clean rule after the fact.
execution_policy_NON_NEGOTIABLE.5_drafts_only: Automation DRAFTS a new dashboard version; it does NOT silently overwrite. The user reviews and commits.

freetrade_costs.uk_shares: 0 commission, 0.5% stamp duty on buys (REITs/ITs/ETFs exempt)
freetrade_costs.note: FX fee applies to US/EU trade legs only - it is NOT part of mark-to-market valuation (use live spot FX for GBP value). No fractional UK shares - whole UK shares only (drives the whole-share RBTX/ARCG DCA ticket).
